world_assessment: Relatively quiet week dominated by crypto/meme noise (Dogecoin surpassing Ford's market cap — irrelevant noise). Two key fundamental signals matter: (1) BofA upgrades NUE and Steel Dynamics citing RECORD steel prices — direct institutional confirmation of our infrastructure materials thesis. (2) MA receives dual price target raises from Morgan Stanley ($418) and Barclays ($402) — consensus aligning around our Digital Finance Revolution thesis. Evercore ISI names 4 clean energy stocks to outperform — potential tailwind for FSLR. Cathie Wood loading up on AMZN, COIN, JPM signals institutional conviction in tech and digital finance. Toyota unveiling EV strategy adds competitive pressure to F's EV narrative. FINRA barring former GS analyst for insider trading is legacy noise, not thesis-relevant. We are now 9.8% behind the S&P — sitting on 24% cash during a bull market is the single biggest drag on our performance. The portfolio's core theses are largely intact but execution risk is in over-holding weak positions and under-deploying capital.

Earnings season is live — JPM Q1 results are the next thesis inflection point. Blue Wave Reflation thesis (rising NIM, stimulus-driven capital markets) remains intact. Below SMA50 is a concern but stop not threatened. Hold through earnings.

Capital markets thesis (M&A surge, IPO pipeline, trading revenues) intact. Q1 earnings will be the key inflection. Hold.

Stop at $8.50 gives wide buffer from $11.73. Flagging for close watch — need to see stabilization above SMA50 or a legislative catalyst soon.

MACD bearish and OBV slightly falling may reflect post-upgrade profit-taking. Core thesis confirmed — hold.

No specific negative news. Stop at $46 gives buffer. Technically weak — monitoring, but commodity reflation thesis intact.

RSI=50, above SMA50, MACD bearish, OBV flat. Neutral technicals — neither confirming nor denying thesis. Rising rate NIM expansion thesis and retail investor surge thesis both intact. No specific news. Hold.

Two independent major institutions raising targets simultaneously is strong consensus signal. RSI=60, above SMA50, MACD bullish, OBV rising — technically the cleanest setup in the portfolio right now. Payment network toll-road thesis getting institutional validation. Highest near-term conviction this week.

If price breaks below $14 or OBV continues accelerating downward, will consider proactive exit before stop.

theme_updates.name: Digital Finance Revolution
theme_updates.reason: MA receives dual institutional price target increases in the same week (MS to $418, Barclays to $402), and Cathie Wood is publicly accumulating COIN (Coinbase, newly public crypto exchange). Two independent data points — analyst consensus and ARK capital flows — confirm accelerating institutional conviction in digital payments and crypto infrastructure. Incrementing from 5 to... wait, already at 5/5 maximum. No change possible.

lesson_updates.reason: MA receiving simultaneous price target upgrades from two independent major institutions (Morgan Stanley and Barclays) in the same week is a form of fundamental confirmation analogous to an earnings beat with above-consensus guidance. The principle — size up after confirmation if technical conditions and allocation limits allow — is again validated. We cannot add here due to cash constraints, but the signal is identical to what Lesson 4 describes. Incrementing to 5/5.

belief_updates.name: Rate-Regime Portfolio Alignment
belief_updates.description: The interest rate regime is the single most important macro variable for sector allocation. In a rising-rate environment: actively overweight financials (NIM expansion), energy (real asset pricing power), and cyclicals (operating leverage); actively underweight or avoid long-duration assets (clean energy utilities, high-multiple growth tech) which face valuation compression from rising discount rates. The rate environment can overwhelm a fundamentally sound company-level thesis for multiple quarters. Execute rotation preemptively when the rate trend is confirmed — not reactively after compression has occurred. Signal to act: 10-year yield in sustained uptrend with Fed taper/hike language.

belief_updates.name: Conviction Compounds on Confirmation
belief_updates.description: When a core position receives fundamental confirmation — earnings beat with above-consensus guidance, simultaneous price target raises from multiple independent major institutions, or direct policy catalysts materializing — increase position size if (1) technical conditions support entry (RSI not extended, MACD bullish or neutral, above SMA50) and (2) allocation limits allow. Confirmation reduces asymmetric uncertainty and increases thesis conviction. The optimal behavior is to add at confirmation, not wait for the next confirmation. Failure to act on confirmation is a form of opportunity cost that compounds across multiple positions.

weekly_summary: Week of April 21, 2021: Portfolio $101,044 (+1.0%) vs S&P +10.9%, trailing by -9.8%. Two major thesis confirmations: BofA upgrades NUE citing record steel prices (Blue Wave Reflation materials confirmed, actual gain ~+30% not -35% as ledger error suggests); MA receives dual PT raises from MS ($418) and Barclays ($402) in the same week (strongest validation signal in the portfolio this week). No new positions opened — only ~$4K deployable above 20% cash floor, below minimum 5% position threshold. SOFI remains the portfolio's most technically deteriorated position (RSI=36, confirmed downtrend ADX=34, OBV falling) — on proactive watch ahead of stop at $12. F technicals weakening with Toyota EV competition adding narrative pressure. Two new Beliefs elevated from high-scoring lessons: Rate-Regime Portfolio Alignment (from Lesson 7, score 5) and Conviction Compounds on Confirmation (from Lesson 4, score 5). Earnings season now the key catalyst for JPM, GS thesis validation.
